findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Over-broad keyword baiting in skill description
findings.description: The skill description contains an extensive list of trigger keywords including 'add MCP server', 'integrate MCP', 'configure MCP in plugin', 'use .mcp.json', 'set up Model Context Protocol', 'connect external service', and '${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} with MCP'. This broad keyword coverage increases the likelihood of unintended activation, potentially inflating the skill's perceived capabilities beyond its intended scope.
findings.evidence: Description: 'This skill should be used when the user asks to "add MCP server", "integrate MCP", "configure MCP in plugin", "use .mcp.json", "set up Model Context Protocol", "connect external service", mentions "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} with MCP", or discusses MCP server types (SSE, stdio, HTTP, WebSocket).'
findings.remediation: Reduce the number of trigger keywords to only the most essential ones. Avoid including generic terms like 'connect external service' that could trigger the skill in unrelated contexts.

findings.title: Missing optional metadata fields
findings.description: The skill manifest is missing the optional 'allowed-tools' and 'compatibility' fields. While these are optional per the spec, their absence provides no explicit tool restrictions or compatibility information, which is a minor documentation gap.
findings.evidence: YAML frontmatter only includes: name, description, version. Missing: allowed-tools, compatibility, license.
findings.remediation: Add 'allowed-tools' and 'compatibility' fields to the YAML frontmatter to explicitly declare tool usage boundaries and platform compatibility.

findings.title: Potential for misleading instructions regarding wildcard tool permissions
findings.description: The skill documentation mentions wildcard tool pre-allowing ('mcp__plugin_asana_asana__*') with a caution to 'use sparingly', but does not provide strong enough warnings against this practice. Wildcard permissions could lead to unintended tool access if a skill is compromised.
findings.evidence: Section: 'Wildcard (use sparingly):' with example 'allowed-tools: ["mcp__plugin_asana_asana__*"]' and note 'Best practice: Pre-allow specific tools, not wildcards, for security.'
findings.remediation: Remove the wildcard example entirely or add a stronger warning that wildcard permissions should never be used in production.
